programme.desc: Police raid a house in search of drugs, but find something terrifying once inside the property. One of the most unusual properties ever goes on sale In a Midlands Proceeds of Crime auction - a 'hidden house'. Plus, a chunk of money made from selling criminal assets is given over to a football club to help them train young local players(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: At Southwell, Nottinghamshire, comedian Joe Pasquale and ventriloquist Paul Zerdin take on Debbie McGee and Sooty puppeteer Richard Cadell in a bid to raise money for Children in Need. Experts Tim Weeks and Thomas Forrester assist them in their endeavours, while Charles Hanson wields his gavel at what promises to be a corker of an auction. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen visits Martha in hospital, while Daniel is faced with a vision of the future. Sid gets suspicious after attending a fall at work(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a primary school teacher, a legal writer and a retired telemarketer must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades visits Altrincham Recycling Centre in search of three items of tip-bound trash that he can rework and repurpose to reveal their true potential. With three curious items saved, Jay enlists two specialist makers to turn trash into cash. Designer Daniel Heath gives a garden chiminea a radical redesign, and upcycler Sarah Peterson gives a shabby set of curtains a style overhaul. But will their transformations be able to make some money for nothing?(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and James Braxton head through Somerset, Devon and Dorset in search of bargain, before they face their final auction in Dorchester and the winner is declared(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances, in the lead-up to Children in Need. Plus, the latest updates on the Rickshaw Challenge(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Graham Norton, Tess Daly, Mel Giedroyc and Ade Adepitan introduce guest appearances from the nation's most-loved celebrities, helping raise money for disadvantaged children and young people across the UK. Including an EastEnders edition of Strictly Come Dancing, in which Ricky Champ, Louisa Lytton, Maisie Smith and Rudolph Walker compete for the Pudsey Glitterball. Ricky is partnered with Luba Mushtuk, Louisa with Gorka Marquez, Maisie with Kevin Clifton and Rudolph with Nancy Wu. Plus, studio performances from West End shows Big and Circus 1903, the Children in Need Choir singing live up and down the country, a visit from Doctor Who, a Star Wars spectacular, the long-awaited return of children's favourite Crackerjack, Countryfile presenters as never witnessed before and a special surprise courtesy of the England football team. Featuring regular reports of fundraising efforts around the regions(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Marvin and Rochelle Humes host the annual charity fundraiser, featuring music from Westlife as well as a special track from the Children in Need Got It Covered album and the West End cast of Tina Turner the Musical performing live. Plus, special versions of The Hit List and Mock the Week, children taking control of Graham Norton's red chair and Pudsey jetting off to meet some fundraisers in Cyprus(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones, Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates are tasked to create advertising campaigns to entice people from the UK to visit Finland during the summer months. Half the team head to Finland to film an advert for television, whilst the others create a marketing campaign and a digital billboard to be displayed at London's Piccadilly circus. Next, the candidates pitch their campaigns to leading industry experts, including the Head of Tourism for Finland. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Glynn Purnell is joined by chefs Marcus Samuelsson and Richard Bainbridge, and special guest Sophie Ellis-Bextor. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein and Keith Floyd(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Four colleagues from the Tron Theatre in Glasgow compete. Business development manager Lucy is joined by front of house manager Khaliq, marketing officer Olivia and manager Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, as the Premier League and Championship make way for the latest international matches(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Halesowen in Birmingham and an end terrace in Northwood in Stoke on Trent are sold under the hammer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Comedian Joe Pasquale and ventriloquist Paul Zerdin take on Debbie McGee and Sooty puppeteer Richard Cadell in a bid to raise money for Children in Need. Experts Tim Weeks and Thomas Forrester assist them in their endeavours, while Natasha Raskin Sharp presents(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: Barbarians v Fiji (Kick-off 2.30pm). John Inverdale presents coverage of the fixture from Twickenham, as the invitational side face the Pacific Islanders. The Barbarians' unique blend of players from around the world are often easy on the eye, while Fiji - fresh from giving Wales a scare at the World Cup in October - will be keen to make an impact. With studio analysis from Martin Johnson and Ben Ryan, and commentary by Eddie Butler and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers help Naomi and Chris in Burton on Trent, who struggle with their steeply sloping plot and love their motorbikes more than their garden(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, sisters Sara and Vanessa from London, friends John and Rachel from Glasgow, and father and son duo Fluff and Freddie from Kent take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the celebrity couples take to the dancefloor at Blackpool's Tower Ballroom - minus Mike Bushell and Katya Jones, who were eliminated last weekend. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the final episode of the series, Carol and Aimee, a mother and daughter from Bristol, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A routine CQC investigation has huge ramifications for both the staff and patients at the hospital. Meanwhile, Robyn is devastated when she realises she cannot rely on Duffy, Ethan stifles his feelings and turns his back on Effie, and Rash protects Mason from Dylan. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working on a cattle ranch in Austin, Texas, where he gets to grips with farm machinery and faces one of his biggest fears, horses(n)

programme.title: Flog It!

The experts also appraise a Tongan war club that narrowly escaped being chopped up for firewood and a 1960s TV set inspired by the first Moon landing(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Documentary examining the life and career of one of cinema's most glamorous stars, using rarely seen archive material to tell the story of her hugely successful career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A profile of the screen career of John Hurt, the celebrated British character actor who died last month, having made a name for himself via roles in films such as The Elephant Man, The Naked Civil Servant, Midnight Express and 1984. In a selection of rare archive interviews, John discusses some of his most iconic cinematic creations, his perspective on acting, and the tragedy that claimed the life of the woman he planned to marry(n)

programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day five from The O2 in London, featuring a singles match in the round-robin stage. Three veterans of this tournament in Rafael Nadal, Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer have qualified for his tournament, while in contrast, Daniil Medvedev and Stefanos Tsitsipas secured their places in the world's top eight to qualify for this competition for the first time(n)

After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - William Sitwell, Tracey MacLeod and Amol Rajan. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round(n)

programme.desc: In the aftermath of the gunfight, Kenzo learns that Yuto's shrapnel-ridden body has been taken to Sarah's flat, so he, Vickers and Donna rush there and attempt makeshift surgery in order to save his brother's life. But also under the knife is Fukuhara in Tokyo, his bullet wounds threatening to crumble the very foundations of the Yakuza peace treaty. Crime thriller, starring Takehiro Hira, Yuko Nakamora and Kelly Macdonald(n)

programme.desc: Ronnie Wood makes his debut on the show, with his Wild Five Band featuring Imelda May on vocals. Scottish chart-topper Lewis Capaldi sings tracks from his album Divinely Uninspired to a Hellish Extent and Oxford rockers Foals make a welcome return to the studio after a busy year. programme.title: Newsnight

programme.desc: As a glamorous profile of Asma appears in Vogue magazine, the Arab Spring protests reach Syria. Bashar must decide whether to concede to their demands for greater freedom or follow in his father Hafez's famously hardline steps. What unfolds shocks the world in its brutality, but as the chaos of civil war rages, the so-called Islamic State emerges and it looks like Bashar is on the brink of losing the Assad dynasty's control of Syria(n)

programme.desc: Monty Don plants out berried shrubs to help feed wildlife in the winter, harvests pumpkins and shows how to make use of fallen autumnal leaves. Carol Klein shows the planting partners still making an impact in the borders of the Bishop's Palace and Gardens in Somerset, while Adam Frost is in the Cotswolds finding out about some surprising planting schemes designed with animals in mind. Frances Tophill summarises her first season on her allotment in Kent and Rachel de Thame updates her containers to ensure that they give interest for the upcoming seasons ahead(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A tribute to the actor, featuring interviews recorded at key stages in his career on stage and screen. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day six from The O2 in London, featuring the penultimate singles match of the round-robin stage. Eventual champion Alexander Zverev booked his semi-final spot in last year's corresponding contest with a 7-6, 6-3 victory over John Isner(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: As all the couples head to Blackpool, Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress. Gethin Jones will be at the Blackpool Tower chatting to the couples, while Zoe and Rylan keep the sofa warm in the Two studio along with some celebrity super fans, who deliver their verdict on recent performances(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are the Cunard shipping line, Strictly Come Dancing, the life and films of Ingrid Bergman and rock band Manic Street Preachers. This is followed by a two-minute general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: The work of nine mountain vets, several nurses and stable hands in three very different veterinary practices located within the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland, as they care for a diverse range of animals in clinics, on farms and smallholdings. The Mourne area is well known for its stunning natural beauty and traditional country life, and the trio of practices respond to over 100 clinic cases and call-outs a day. In this episode, the founder of the oldest vet practice in Downpatrick, Maurice King, receives a call about an extraordinarily rare birth on a farm he knows well(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: The abbot welcomes the delegates to the conference with a lavish supper. Bernard Gui asks to be introduced to William's novice Adso and takes a particular interest when told he is German, believing him to be a spy sent by the emperor. Salvatore warns Remigio that they should flee since he is certain that Gui has recognised him. In the woods outside the abbey, Anna is found wounded and unconscious in the woods by the Occitan girl, who tends to her injuries, and is later visited by Adso. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Greta Scarano star(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti visit a hotel in South Africa that has been built into a former grain silo, and discover how the staff cope in the wake of a severe drought(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Current Dragons Peter Jones, Tej Lalvani, Touker Suleyman and Sara Davies are joined by former Dragon Sarah Willingham, dining out at the I'll Get This restaurant in this special episode. However, rather than one of them paying the bill, they'll all be making donations to Children in Need, with the amounts increasing as the evening progresses(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Extended edition of the music programme. Ronnie Wood and Imelda May perform tunes from Ronnie's latest album Mad Lad: A Live Tribute to Chuck Berry, as well as sharing hosting duties with Jools and chatting to Scottish chart-topper Lewis Capaldi about love and success. Plus, live performances from Capaldi, Oxford rockers Foals, singer-songwriter Nilufer Yanya and violinist, singer and songwriter Sudan Archives(n)

programme.title: Look Back in Anger (1959)
programme.desc: A disillusioned cynic takes out his anger at the world by having an affair with his wife's best friend, causing untold misery for everyone he knows. Tony Richardson's adaptation of John Osborne's play, starring Richard Burton, Mary Ure and Claire Bloom(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Justin Rowlatt investigates the aviation industry's plans to reduce carbon emissions and help save the planet, asking whether it is promising more than it is delivering. With cheap flights leading to a boom in passenger numbers, he hears claims that the industry is putting growth and profits before the environment(n)

programme.title: The Day California Burned
programme.desc: Documentary examining the devastating wildfire that swept through northern California in November 2018, causing the deaths of more than 80 people and approximately $16.5billion of damage. The film features interviews with firefighters, police officers and local residents who came face to face with the fire, including a nurse who evacuated her patients before finding herself trapped in the middle of the blaze(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia encounters a passive-aggressive lift(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby League
programme.desc: Papua New Guinea v Great Britain (Kick-off 7.30am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the fourth and final Test for the Lions, which takes place at Oil Search National Football Stadium in Port Moresby. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall continues his quest to encounter the world's 60 deadliest animals in their natural habitats. Heading to South Africa, he goes in search of the great white shark and has a trick up his sleeve to demonstrate its hunting prowess. The crew also observes cheetahs, a trio of the nation's most venomous snakes and the powerful secretary bird(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the final, featuring the five heat winners and one of the runners-up. Judges Sethu Vijayakumar, Noel Sharkey and Dr Lucy Rodgers decide the winner, while Jonathan Pearce provides commentary on the mayhem(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dr Ranj Singh and Dr Zoe Williams go looking for antiques in Lancashire and Greater Manchester, aided by experts Stephanie Connell and Tim Medhurst. Ranj visits a Blackpool dance hall and Zoe joins the crew of a steam train before attending an auction in Harrogate, where they hope to make a profit on their purchases(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the latest heat, Monica Galetti challenges three of the chefs to employ deft knife skills to spatchcock a quail before cooking it with rocket pesto in just 15 minutes. The remaining contestants prepare Marcus Wareing's beignets with chocolate sauce, before all six get the chance to redeem any earlier mistakes when they cook their own signature dish for Monica, Marcus and Gregg Wallace. The judges then decide which three have done enough to make it through to the quarter-final stage of the competition(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three chefs recreate Monica's razor clams with a shallot, caper and tomato sauce, before the second group of three take on Marcus' calves liver with a Bordelaise sauce. In the next round, the six chefs aim to impress the judges with a dish of their own design, with just one hour and 15 minutes to prepare it(n)

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage from day seven at the O2 in London, featuring the opening semi-final of the singles competition. Twelve months ago, record six-time winner Roger Federer suffered a shock straight-sets defeat to Germany's Alexander Zverev, while Serbian Novak Djokovic overcame South Africa's Kevin Anderson to reach the final. Analysis and commentary comes from Tim Henman, Andrew Castle and Boris Becker(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: Papua New Guinea v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents highlights of the fourth and final Test for the Lions, which took place at Oil Search National Football Stadium in Port Moresby. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: After three months at sea, the test pilots from the Royal Navy and RAF have successfully landed the F35 jets on the flight deck of HMS Queen Elizabeth for the first time, and even tested launching and landing in hurricane conditions. But with just a few weeks to go before the warship arrives in New York in spectacular style, they are about to try a method of landing never attempted before, which carries great risks but could revolutionise carrier operations globally. Narrated by Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring lands himself in trouble with Warden Hodges, accused of leaving a light burning in the church hall during a blackout, and receives a summons to appear in court. Comedy first aired in 1972, starring Arthur Lowe and Bill Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: Documentary following Welsh wildlife. The first day of spring has a sting in its tail as snow still covers the land. These are challenging times for the Carneddau pony, an ancient breed that lives on the tops of Snowdonia's highest peaks. As the sun slowly strengthens, adders head off on the hunt, dippers begin nest building and black grouse fight for the right to mate(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from Canberra, the seat of government, to the multi-ethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Along the way he discovers the history of the Ashes at Melbourne cricket ground, learns about the exploits of outlaw Ned Kelly and visits the home of superstar soprano Dame Nellie Melba. He also rides on the Puffing Billy narrow gauge steam railway, built in 1900, and has a go at shearing a sheep(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'quacks' and 'quackery', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Victoria Coren Mitchell, Stephen K Amos and Claudia Winkleman. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: The second documentary examining fictional writing from three different perspectives considers the subject of race and colonialism. It looks at books including Robinson Crusoe, the first novel ever published in English, in 1719, whose hero was a slave trader, and two famous American novels published more than a century apart - Harriet Beecher Stowe's Uncle Tom's Cabin and Toni Morrison's Beloved. The programme also comes up to date with titles such as Malorie Blackman's Noughts and Crosses and Paul Beatty's The Sell Out(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: The House of Evangelista are set to kill two birds with one stone, as they plan a press stunt aimed at raising Aids awareness and antagonising Frederica at the same time. Meanwhile, Angel is introduced to the less savoury sides of modelling. As her big break approaches, will she be able to stay on track?(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: There's turmoil in the House of Evangelista as Damon - feeling betrayed by Ricky - turns on his housemates and reveals everyone else's secrets. Blanca tries to manage the crisis as best she can, but even she may not be as impartial as she thinks she is(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The Rosetta mission to comet 67P was the first time a spacecraft landed on a comet's surface. Cameras reveal what this icy body taught us about the dawn of the solar system and the origins of life on Earth(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox embarks on a globe-trotting quest to explain how the galactic forces that formed the solar system also shaped Earth's landscape. In the first programme, he examines the sun's rule over the planet, heading for India to witness a total solar eclipse, and viewing the Northern Lights in the Arctic Circle(n)

programme.title: Climategate: Science of a Scandal
programme.desc: Documentary investigating a data breach at the University of East Anglia in 2009 that suggested scientists had manipulated data to exaggerate evidence of climate change(n)

programme.title: Unlocking Nature's Secrets: The Serengeti Rules
programme.desc: Five veteran scientists tell the stories of their adventures in the wilderness going back to the 1960s, when their insatiable curiosity for the way nature worked took them to some of the most spectacular and remote places on Earth, from the majestic Serengeti and the Amazon jungle to the Arctic Ocean and Pacific tide pools(n)

programme.title: Climate Change by Numbers
programme.desc: The debate surrounding climate change, as the public appears to be less sure it understands exactly what is going on, despite there being more information on the issue than ever. Mathematicians Dr Hannah Fry, Professor Norman Fenton and Professor David Spiegelhalter discuss the three key numbers that clarify the important questions around climate change, as scientists use them to help explain what is happening to the Earth(n)

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Examining the tremendous changes wrought in Africa between the 15th and 18th centuries, including the seismic transformation as West African kingdoms encounter European mariners travelling farther and farther south along Africa's Atlantic coast(n)

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr brings the story into the 19th century, when a fierce competition for resources and trade stimulated ingenuity, while also enticing European powers and inciting conflicts that would threaten the stability and well-being of the continent(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox embarks on a globe-trotting quest to explain how the galactic forces that formed the solar system also shaped Earth's landscape. In the first programme, he examines the sun's rule over the planet, heading for India to witness a total solar eclipse, and viewing the Northern Lights in the Arctic Circle(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Caron Keating present the edition first aired on October 20, featuring performances by D Mob featuring Gary Haisman, the Christians, Enya, Deacon Blue, Milli Vanilli, Erasure, Kylie Minogue, Whitney Houston and The Wee Papa Girl Rappers(n)

programme.title: A Blackpool Big Band Special: Jools Holland and His Rhythm & Blues Orchestra
programme.desc: The pianist and presenter of Later leads his orchestra in a concert at Blackpool's Empress Ballroom celebrating the golden age of big band music from the 1930s-50s, with interpretations of standards by the likes of Count Basie, Louis Armstrong and Woody Herman. Highlights include singer-songwriter Rumer's take on Ac-Cent-Tchu-Ate the Positive, and Marc Almond performing a rendition of Edith Piaf's Hymne a l'amour(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Anthea Turner present the edition first aired on October 27, featuring performances by Milli Vanilli, the Art of Noise and Tom Jones, Royal House, Tanita Tikaram, Robert Palmer, Yazz, Deacon Blue, Enya, and the Beatmasters with PP Arnold(n)

programme.title: Blue Note Records: Beyond the Notes
programme.desc: When German-Jewish refugees Alfred Lion and Francis Wolff started Blue Note in 1939 in New York, the duo allowed artists complete freedom and encouraged them to compose new music. Their visionary and uncompromising approach led to releases that not only revolutionized jazz, but also left an indelible imprint on art and music, including hip-hop. This documentary delves behind the scenes to look at the pioneering label that gave voice to some of the finest jazz artists of the 20th and 21st centuries. Featuring rare archival interviews with Blue Note musicians(n)

programme.title: David Bowie: Finding Fame
programme.desc: The final part of Francis Whately's trilogy of films on Bowie's life, telling the story of the five years when many of the ideas that would help him become an icon were first born. Starting in 1966, soon after David Jones changed his name to David Bowie, the film traces his interest in everything from Holst and Pinky and Perky to Anthony Newley and Tibetan Buddhism, and how he used all of these influences to create not only Ziggy Stardust, but the material for his entire career(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: In the wake of the First World War, former sapper Thomas Shelby and his brothers return from the trenches to their hometown of Birmingham, where their family runs a feared crime ring - making money from illegal betting, protection and the black market. Thomas recognises an opportunity to move up in the world when a crate of guns goes missing from an arms factory, but government minister Winston Churchill sends in a ruthless police chief from Belfast to impose order on the increasingly lawless city and recover the weapons. First episode of the drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Chief Inspector Campbell carries out a violent raid on the gang's territory and demands a meeting with Thomas, who is later sent a bullet with his name on it after picking a fight with a gypsy family at a country fair. Meanwhile, Aunt Polly is shocked to discover Ada is pregnant, and when Thomas discovers his former war comrade Freddie Thorne is the father, he devises a plan to get his old friend out of town. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Two men claiming to be IRA sympathisers approach Thomas in the Garrison and offer to buy the missing crate of guns they have heard he has in his possession. The gang leader also goes to Cheltenham races with Grace, where he aims to persuade kingpin Billy Kimber to make the Peaky Blinders part of his operation, while Ada secretly marries communist agitator Freddie Thorne. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: James May's Big Trouble in Model Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following a year at British toymaker Hornby Hobbies as it teeters on the brink of collapse. Director Simon Kohler assesses the state of his beloved business after his four-year absence, while master modeller Jim is given the biggest build of his career(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: The first of three programmes in which cameras explore some of the most inhospitable places on the planet and the people who live there, including a team of Sherpa roping a route on Mount Everest's notorious Khumbu Icefall and, on the slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro, farmers fight pitched battles to save their crops from marauding elephants. In the Amazon rainforest, two boys undergo a rite of passage involving hundreds of ants with the most painful stings on Earth, and conservationists in the Grand Canyon try to ensure the survival of one of America's few surviving condor chicks(n)

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In Newham, east London, the Trident team is targeting one of the capital's most dangerous gangs, whose leaders groom school children to commit violent acts of crime - and DCI Jim McKee gets a lead after the kidnap and torture of a teenage boy. New Scotland Yard appeal for witnesses to come forward when a man falls victim to an attack in Trafalgar Square, and dies later from his injuries. In Ealing, response officers Naomi Pearce and Mahad Abdalla deal with a drunk-driver(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Graham Norton, Tess Daly, Mel Giedroyc and Ade Adepitan introduce guest appearances from the nation's most-loved celebrities, helping raise money for disadvantaged children and young people. Including an EastEnders edition of Strictly Come Dancing, in which Ricky Champ, Louisa Lytton, Maisie Smith and Rudolph Walker compete for the Pudsey Glitterball. Plus, studio performances from West End shows Big and Circus 1903, the Children in Need Choir singing live up and down the country, a visit from Doctor Who, a Star Wars spectacular, the long-awaited return of children's favourite Crackerjack, Countryfile presenters as never witnessed before and a special surprise courtesy of the England football team. In Wales Sean Fletcher and Sophie Evans are joined by a host of special guests including singer-songwriter Paul Carrack, and report on how past donations have been used at 8.00, 8.45 and 9.30(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Marvin and Rochelle Humes host the annual charity fundraiser, featuring music from Westlife as well as a special track from the Children in Need Got It Covered album and the West End cast of Tina Turner the Musical performing live. Plus, special versions of The Hit List and Mock the Week, children taking control of Graham Norton's red chair and Pudsey jetting off to meet some fundraisers in Cyprus(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones, Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates are tasked to create advertising campaigns to entice people from the UK to visit Finland during the summer months. Half the team head to Finland to film an advert for television, whilst the others create a marketing campaign and a digital billboard to be displayed at London's Piccadilly circus. Next, the candidates pitch their campaigns to leading industry experts, including the Head of Tourism for Finland. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: Barbarians v Fiji (Kick-off 2.30pm). John Inverdale presents coverage of the fixture from Twickenham, as the invitational side face the Pacific Islanders. The Barbarians' unique blend of players from around the world are often easy on the eye, while Fiji - fresh from giving Wales a scare at the World Cup in October - will be keen to make an impact. With studio analysis from Martin Johnson and Ben Ryan, and commentary by Eddie Butler and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out on a quest to discover the truth about rugby. It's a story of vodka, Groggs and adventures away, full of lively characters from the nation's sporting past.(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: This walk takes Derek Brockway to the remote Berwyn mountains, where he walks from Llangynog to the spectacular Pistyll Rhaeadr waterfall, one of the seven wonders of Wales(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Ade Edmondson, Beattie Edmondson, Helen Richardson-Walsh, Kate Richardson-Walsh, Dr Ranj Singh, Min Singh, Mark Steel and Elliot Steel(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, sisters Sara and Vanessa from London, friends John and Rachel from Glasgow, and father and son duo Fluff and Freddie from Kent take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the celebrity couples take to the dancefloor at Blackpool's Tower Ballroom - minus Mike Bushell and Katya Jones, who were eliminated last weekend. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the final episode of the series, Carol and Aimee, a mother and daughter from Bristol, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A routine CQC investigation has huge ramifications for both the staff and patients at the hospital. Meanwhile, Robyn is devastated when she realises she cannot rely on Duffy, Ethan stifles his feelings and turns his back on Effie, and Rash protects Mason from Dylan. Starring Amanda Henderson, Cathy Shipton and Derek Thompson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Azerbaijan v Wales. Jason Mohammad presents highlights of the Euro 2020 Group E qualifying match from Eighth Kilometre District Stadium in Baku, where Wales will have been expecting three points against the group minnows(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: Wyn and Charlotte are proud to support a new feature film shooting in Wales, but discover it's not quite the sort of movie they thought it was. programme.desc: As more people worry about the use of single-use plastic, cameras follow the Sainsbury's innovation team as they try to find a recyclable alternative for the plastic bag used to carry loose fruit and vegetables. But as it is launched across the country, are customers ready for such a change in shopping habits? With Easter around the corner, the supply staff decide what to stock in each store and how much, while at the clothing HQ, plans are already being drawn up for this year's Christmas fashion(n)

programme.desc: Undercover body transformation programme in which lifestyle and fitness experts Rab and Stephen team up with two volunteers who want to lose weight and improve their fitness. However, there's a twist. programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Cyprus v Scotland. Jonathan Sutherland presents action the Euro 2020 Group I qualifier at GSP Stadium. Scotland are looking to complete a double over the Cypriots, having won 2-1 when the sides met at Hampden Park in June(n)

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins the coastguard of Corpus Christi, Texas, taking to the skies with a helicopter patrol and joining a boat crew looking for illegal fishing and smuggling(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of properties sold at auction in Gorleston in Norfolk, Dover in Kent and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Gang members wrongly think they can cash in by deliberately causing a collision with a bus, while a holidaymaker's claim for lost luggage is grounded thanks to forged documents(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Thames Valley Police pursue suspect fraudsters in their region and give one man a very early morning call. A drug dealer loses his monster truck at auction - along with a whole host of movie props. Plus, how money seized from drug dealers is helping to keep football fans safe in the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by John Cameron and Gary Pe in Malvern, Worcestershire, and a regular Bargain Hunt expert talks about his love of Whitefriars Glass(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara saves the day when Martha has an allergic reaction, while Karen is shocked to discover how it happened. Rob deals with an elderly man behaving like a lovestruck teenager(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the penultimate episode of the quiz in which contestants choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is on a property-hunting mission in the Dorset countryside, helping a couple who have a £750,000 budget with which to buy a home, a holiday let and land(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three more items from a tip in Witley, Surrey, transforming a redundant car bonnet, an old wax jacket and a dated desk(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and James Braxton explore Somerset, travelling from Glastonbury to Frome via Exmoor. James hopes an antibacterial purchase will help him clean up at auction(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel and Sharon desperately try to get to Phil first. Will Sharon be able thwart Mel's plan and keep the secret that will tear her family apart hidden - or is the truth about to explode? Chantelle confronts Gray about the fight he had, but she is not convinced by his answer(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: As more people worry about the use of single-use plastic, cameras follow the Sainsbury's innovation team as they try to find a recyclable alternative for the plastic bag used to carry loose fruit and vegetables. But as it is launched across the country, are customers ready for such a change in shopping habits? With Easter around the corner, the supply staff decide what to stock in each store and how much, while at the clothing HQ, plans are already being drawn up for this year's Christmas fashion(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In Newham, east London, the Trident team is targeting one of the capital's most dangerous gangs, whose leaders groom school children to commit violent acts of crime - and DCI Jim McKee gets a lead after the kidnap and torture of a teenage boy. New Scotland Yard appeal for witnesses to come forward when a man falls victim to an attack in Trafalgar Square, and dies later from his injuries. In Ealing, response officers Naomi Pearce and Mahad Abdalla deal with a drunk-driver(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Brighton, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US Marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell views a two-bed semi in Guildford, Surrey, Dion Dublin visits Ash in Kent to look around a derelict barn, while Martin Roberts is in Kidsgrove, Staffordshire(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A genuine bus accident that could have had tragic consequences leads to an outrageous claim for compensation, and a prisoner trips up with a bogus personal injury claim(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police raid a house in search of drugs, but find something terrifying once inside the property. One of the most unusual properties ever goes on sale In a Midlands Proceeds of Crime auction - a 'hidden house'. Plus, a chunk of money made from selling criminal assets is given over to a football club to help them train young local players(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: At Southwell, Nottinghamshire, comedian Joe Pasquale and ventriloquist Paul Zerdin take on Debbie McGee and Sooty puppeteer Richard Cadell in a bid to raise money for Children in Need. Experts Tim Weeks and Thomas Forrester assist them in their endeavours, while Charles Hanson wields his gavel at what promises to be a corker of an auction. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen visits Martha in hospital, while Daniel is faced with a vision of the future. Sid gets suspicious after attending a fall at work(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a primary school teacher, a legal writer and a retired telemarketer must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades visits Altrincham Recycling Centre in search of three items of tip-bound trash that he can rework and repurpose to reveal their true potential. With three curious items saved, Jay enlists two specialist makers to turn trash into cash. Designer Daniel Heath gives a garden chiminea a radical redesign, and upcycler Sarah Peterson gives a shabby set of curtains a style overhaul. But will their transformations be able to make some money for nothing?(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and James Braxton head through Somerset, Devon and Dorset in search of bargain, before they face their final auction in Dorchester and the winner is declared(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances, in the lead-up to Children in Need. Plus, the latest updates on the Rickshaw Challenge(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Graham Norton, Tess Daly, Mel Giedroyc and Ade Adepitan introduce guest appearances from the nation's most-loved celebrities, helping raise money for disadvantaged children and young people across the UK. Including an EastEnders edition of Strictly Come Dancing, in which Ricky Champ, Louisa Lytton, Maisie Smith and Rudolph Walker compete for the Pudsey Glitterball. Ricky is partnered with Luba Mushtuk, Louisa with Gorka Marquez, Maisie with Kevin Clifton and Rudolph with Nancy Wu. Plus, studio performances from West End shows Big and Circus 1903, the Children in Need Choir singing live up and down the country, a visit from Doctor Who, a Star Wars spectacular, the long-awaited return of children's favourite Crackerjack, Countryfile presenters as never witnessed before and a special surprise courtesy of the England football team. Featuring regular reports of fundraising efforts around the regions(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Marvin and Rochelle Humes host the annual charity fundraiser, featuring music from Westlife as well as a special track from the Children in Need Got It Covered album and the West End cast of Tina Turner the Musical performing live. Plus, special versions of The Hit List and Mock the Week, children taking control of Graham Norton's red chair and Pudsey jetting off to meet some fundraisers in Cyprus(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones, Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates are tasked to create advertising campaigns to entice people from the UK to visit Finland during the summer months. Half the team head to Finland to film an advert for television, whilst the others create a marketing campaign and a digital billboard to be displayed at London's Piccadilly circus. Next, the candidates pitch their campaigns to leading industry experts, including the Head of Tourism for Finland. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Glynn Purnell is joined by chefs Marcus Samuelsson and Richard Bainbridge, and special guest Sophie Ellis-Bextor. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein and Keith Floyd(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Four colleagues from the Tron Theatre in Glasgow compete. Business development manager Lucy is joined by front of house manager Khaliq, marketing officer Olivia and manager Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, as the Premier League and Championship make way for the latest international matches(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Halesowen in Birmingham and an end terrace in Northwood in Stoke on Trent are sold under the hammer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Comedian Joe Pasquale and ventriloquist Paul Zerdin take on Debbie McGee and Sooty puppeteer Richard Cadell in a bid to raise money for Children in Need. Experts Tim Weeks and Thomas Forrester assist them in their endeavours, while Natasha Raskin Sharp presents(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: Barbarians v Fiji (Kick-off 2.30pm). John Inverdale presents coverage of the fixture from Twickenham, as the invitational side face the Pacific Islanders. The Barbarians' unique blend of players from around the world are often easy on the eye, while Fiji - fresh from giving Wales a scare at the World Cup in October - will be keen to make an impact. With studio analysis from Martin Johnson and Ben Ryan, and commentary by Eddie Butler and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: Weather Watchers with Barra Best
programme.desc: The weather presenter and journalist examines how climate shapes everyone's lives and looks at the activities that it enables people to participate in(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Ade Edmondson, Beattie Edmondson, Helen Richardson-Walsh, Kate Richardson-Walsh, Dr Ranj Singh, Min Singh, Mark Steel and Elliot Steel(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, sisters Sara and Vanessa from London, friends John and Rachel from Glasgow, and father and son duo Fluff and Freddie from Kent take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the celebrity couples take to the dancefloor at Blackpool's Tower Ballroom - minus Mike Bushell and Katya Jones, who were eliminated last weekend. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the final episode of the series, Carol and Aimee, a mother and daughter from Bristol, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A routine CQC investigation has huge ramifications for both the staff and patients at the hospital. Meanwhile, Robyn is devastated when she realises she cannot rely on Duffy, Ethan stifles his feelings and turns his back on Effie, and Rash protects Mason from Dylan. Starring Amanda Henderson, Cathy Shipton and Derek Thompson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Northern Ireland v Netherlands. Gavin Andrews presents action from the Euro 2020 Group C qualifier from Windsor Park. Michael O'Neill's men gave the Dutch a scare in last month's reverse fixture in Rotterdam when substitute Josh Magennis gave them a 1-0 lead, but Holland fought back with two late goals by Memphis Depay and one from Luuk de Jong. With analysis by Stephen Craigan and commentary from Thomas Kane(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Montenegro (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium. This is England's 1,000th international match and they need at least a draw to mark the occasion by securing their place in the Euro 2020 finals. The reverse fixture saw Gareth Southgate's men claim an emphatic 5-1 victory, when the hosts took a shock lead before Michael Keane equalised, Ross Barkley scored a brace and the prolific duo of Harry Kane and Raheem Sterling added one each. With analysis from Roy Keane, Lee Dixon and Ashley Cole, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Montenegro. Mark Pougatch presents action from the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium. Although England's 100 per cent record in the group came to an end in the Czech Republic last month, they will have been expecting to claim at least the draw they needed to secure their place in the Euro 2020 finals with one match to spare. With analysis from Lee Dixon and Glenn Hoddle, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley(n)

programme.title: Out of Their Skin

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's DNA Journey
programme.desc: Anthony McPartlin and Declan Donnelly may think they know who they are, but in this two-part documentary, the popular presenters uncover their surprising and emotional family histories, using a combination of DNA and genealogy. The shock revelations include military men in both of their families - one a medal-winning war hero, the other a deserter. And as well as delving into the past, Ant and Dec also get to meet DNA relatives they never knew existed(n)

With analysis from AP McCoy and Ruby Walsh, reports by Matt Chapman, Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey, Oli Bell and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a nine-month-old terrier who was found wandering the streets whilst heavily pregnant, before spending time with a three-month-old puppy called Pancake, who is having problems with his eyesight. Elsewhere, a dog arrives at Battersea who was found dumped with a terrible case of mange and an injured leg(n)

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: A look at the busy summer season, when it is all-hands-on-deck as the epic Snowdon Race brings runners from all over the world to the mountain. Farmers Jo and Fiona deal with the frustrations of the season's busy months as unruly pets attack their livestock, while big crowds bring problems for Rob and the mountain rescue team.(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.desc: The latest group of men entering the 'love lift' are a tech-savvy personal trainer, an airline pilot, a magician and musician, and a city banker, all hoping to impress a studio full of single women and win a date - but must first get the 30 female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the star-studded version of the singing contest, in which the line-up of famous faces leave their comfort zones and take to the stage to perform - and while some have proved to be naturals, others have struggled somewhat. Simon Cowell mentors the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger is in charge of the Overs, and Louis Walsh handles the Unders, and as usual the judges are each allowed to send one act through to next week by pushing the golden buzzer, leaving the rest at the mercy of the voting public(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show

Plus, Halsey performs in the studio(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's DNA Journey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Anthony McPartlin and Declan Donnelly embark on a journey to New York to follow the bloodlines of their mothers using DNA analysis and genealogy. There is a surprise for Dec as he finds out he has close family he never knew existed, while Ant's history takes him to the United Nations. After a whirlwind tour of the US, it's back to Ireland to investigate a family secret that Ant's family has been trying to unlock for years(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Montenegro (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium. This is England's 1,000th international match and they need at least a draw to mark the occasion by securing their place in the Euro 2020 finals. The reverse fixture saw Gareth Southgate's men claim an emphatic 5-1 victory, when the hosts took a shock lead before Michael Keane equalised, Ross Barkley scored a brace and the prolific duo of Harry Kane and Raheem Sterling added one each. With analysis from Roy Keane, Lee Dixon and Ashley Cole, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Montenegro. Mark Pougatch presents action from the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium. With analysis from Lee Dixon and Glenn Hoddle, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley(n)

When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. programme.desc: Biopic of Stephen Hawking, exploring the renowned astrophysicist's romance with future wife Jane during their time at university in the 1960s and his initial diagnosis with motor neurone disease, which doctors believed would lead to his death within two years. Undaunted by deteriorating health, he continued his groundbreaking research into the origins of the universe. Starring an Oscar-winning Eddie Redmayne, with Felicity Jones and David Thewlis(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Mark Benton stars as depressed postman Wayne, who sinks to his lowest ebb when a speed-dating night goes terribly wrong. Convinced he needs to make a fresh start, he begins stealing cash from the mail, and finds an ally in 15-year-old illiterate truant Damien, who agrees to help him on his rounds. However, it is not long before the neighbours start questioning their unlikely friendship. With Melanie Hill and Michael Taylor(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: New resident Paul Billerton, who works as a gardener in the local parks, takes a shine to local girl Kirsty. However, Paul confides in Kirsty he is carrying a terrible secret he cannot share, which eventually leads him to the brink of self-destruction. With Robyn Addison and Jodhi May(n)

programme.title: On the Buses

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Preston is found dead at the sacred Crowcall stone circle in Midsomer Mow. Suspicion initially falls on local druids led by high priests Ezra Canning and Leticia Clifford, since Preston's plans to plough Gorse Meadow would have barred their access to the site. Barnaby and Jones are joined by new pathologist Kate Wilding to investigate, and as the case wears on, the inspector becomes convinced greed rather than spiritualism is the motive. Starring Neil Dudgeon, Jason Hughes and Tamzin Malleson(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is assigned to investigate when a chocolate factory owner is murdered during a fox hunt. The case leads him to the sleepy Oxfordshire village of Chigton Green, and is soon complicated by the fatal shooting of a local couple with an apparent connection to the first victim. Endeavour uncovers a vicious campaign of gossip and rumour, and meets a local woman who inspires him to consider his future(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Religious fanatic Steven Mullan is found beaten and drowned in his bath. Lewis and Hathaway discover he had been imprisoned for trying to kill well-known atheist Tom Rattan and had only recently been released. The inspector soon has reason to believe the Rattan family is hiding something from him - but nothing prepares him for the evidence Dr Hobson uncovers at the post-mortem. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star, with guest appearances by Jenny Seagrove and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz feels it is a waste of time trying to get money out of the brewery, Maud worries that Reg plans to put her in a home, and Des ruins Raquel's photo shoot(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf is depressed on his last day at the shop, Reg puts his flat on the market, Raquel is thrilled with Ben's photographs, and Ivy is pleased when Don moves back into her bed(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Turner finds himself in the newspaper, Ruth has unwelcome news for Frank, and Lynn is delighted to convey her bad tidings to Chris(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Scott finds himself a poacher's target, Michael finds he has been made a scapegoat, and Turner discovers something shocking about Shirley's past(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Alf Ventress mounts a large-scale surveillance operation in an effort to track down the wool thief responsible for de-fleecing sheep in Aidensfield. programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Stan volunteer Olive as supervisor for the depot nursery. However, when she breaks her glasses and has to go home for a spare pair the clueless duo are left holding the fort. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Anna Karen(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan believes living at home is cramping his style and resolves to find his own place. Vintage comedy with Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's desire to play the guitar proves too much for his flatmates. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious assault leads the police to foil an attempt to steal antiques from Ashfordly Hall, and Vernon goes all-out to persuade a wealthy woman to make a charitable contribution toward his donkey sanctuary - but the discovery that there is already one in the area forces him to change his plans. Meanwhile, Merton devises a solution to Ventress's retirement worries. Rosemary Leach guest stars(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Carol is on the trail of Turner's secret, Mark discovers he has gone up in the world, and Jack finds he cannot resist Annie's powers of persuasion(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is tormented by memories, Nick's outburst troubles Elizabeth, and Carol disturbs an intimate moment(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg and Maureen set up shop, Alma is shaken when Tanya asks her not to let herself into the flat anymore, and Vera agrees to print Mike's T-shirts(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek arranges a surprise day trip to London for Mavis, Tanya tells Alma to keep her nose out of her private life, and Maud is upset when an offer is made on her house(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a difficult case when he investigates the discovery of a body in a well, and he also has to contend with a new boss, Chief Superintendent Moody, who is dubious as to Lewis' motivation for coming out of retirement. As investigations into the human remains in the well proceed, an avant-garde artist's body is also discovered and at first glance it looks like a tragic drugs overdose, but Hathaway and Lewis quickly recognise the tell tale signs of murder(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The start of a shooting rampage through the streets of London leaves a nightclub peppered with bullets and two revellers dead. With Brooke Kinsella(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are mystified by the murders of a popular couple who were stabbed to death while they slept.
